items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat from Record Highs Amid Rising Yields and Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. equities concluded Friday's trading session lower, pulling back from recent record highs as surging oil prices and climbing Treasury yields led investors to anticipate a potential Federal Reserve interest rate hike later this year. The market also saw widespread profit-taking in the technology sector and caution following the Trump-Xi summit.

items.headline: HCW Biologics Inc. ($HCWB)
items.summary: Led daily gains with a significant percentage increase.

items.headline: P3 Health Partners Inc. ($PIII)
items.summary: Experienced a substantial rally during Friday's trading.

items.headline: Microsoft ($MSFT)
items.summary: Climbed after billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management revealed a significant new position in the software giant.

items.headline: Elong Power Holding Limited ($ELPW)
items.summary: Was among the day's biggest decliners.

items.headline: WORK Medical Technology Group LTD ($WOK)
items.summary: Saw a significant drop in its share price.

items.headline: LanzaTech Global, Inc. ($LNZA)
items.summary: Experienced a sharp decline during Friday's trading.

items.headline: Cerebras Systems ($CBRS)
items.summary: Shed value after its explosive public debut the previous session, indicating profit-taking.

items.headline: Intel ($INTC)
items.summary: Fell as the technology sector experienced widespread profit-taking.

items.headline: Nvidia ($NVDA)
items.summary: Retreated amid broader technology sell-off and news of China blocking its chip sales for review.

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Mount, Fed Rate Hike Probability Rises
items.summary: Rising Treasury yields, with the 30-year U.S. Treasury bond topping 5.1%, and sustained increases in energy costs are reigniting inflation fears. This has led financial market participants to price in a nearly 51% probability of a Federal Reserve interest rate increase as early as December 2026.

items.headline: Energy Sector Bucks Trend Amid Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: While most sectors faced declines, the energy sector advanced 2.32% on Friday, driven by surging oil prices. West Texas Intermediate crude for June delivery settled at $105.42 a barrel, and Brent crude for July delivery reached $109.26 a barrel, primarily due to ongoing geopolitical frictions in the Middle East.

items.headline: Tech Stocks See Widespread Profit-Taking After Recent Rally
items.summary: The technology sector, which has experienced significant gains recently, saw widespread profit-taking. Major players like Intel, Advanced Micro Devices, Micron Technology, and Nvidia all retreated more than 5%. Concerns about the sustainability of the AI-driven rally and rising yields contributed to the sell-off.

items.headline: Jackson Financial (JFN) Q1 Earnings Miss, Market Reacts Favorably
items.summary: Jackson Financial reported first-quarter earnings that missed revenue ($2.90 billion, down 22.6% YoY) and adjusted EPS ($5.15 vs. $5.96 consensus) forecasts. However, the market reacted favorably due to strong demand for retail annuity products, particularly Registered Index-Linked Annuities (RILAs) and a new Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA).

items.headline: China to Prioritize Real Economy; Germany Forecasts Q2 Slowdown
items.summary: China's President Xi Jinping emphasized strengthening and expanding the real economy, with a focus on prioritizing the manufacturing sector. Meanwhile, Germany's Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy forecasts a clear slowdown in the German economy during Q2 2026, citing rising prices, supply chain issues, and uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.
